mnemonics: LAND: Leaching, Acidification, Nutrient Depletion, Dumping. Remember the impact of e-waste on land.
keyPoints: E-waste in landfills can lead to soil contamination through the leaching of heavy metals.
Improper disposal reduces soil fertility by altering its chemical and biological properties.
Landfilling e-waste is a primary method but poses significant long-term environmental risks.
commonMistakes: Assuming all e-waste in landfills is safely contained and cannot leach into the soil.
Believing that land is an infinite resource for waste disposal without long-term consequences.
